evidence: `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:724` still uses `requestBody as unknown as TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput`, bypassing the canonical task input contract at the creation seam.
`supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts:246-281` treats any object-shaped 2xx payload as success unless it contains an explicit error field, so malformed success bodies can still be accepted.

`supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts:90-108` force-casts RPC output and computes retry/header fields without validating `allowed/count/reset_at`, which can produce invalid enforcement decisions from malformed backend responses.

`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/hooks/normalizeTaskDetailsPayload.ts:45-47` resolves display fields from a different precedence chain than `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelPayloadReader.ts:21-28`, creating inconsistent read seams between UI details and task-contract readers.

Shot editor generation flow still relies on a large cross-domain parameter bag: `useGenerationController` defines separate `core/prompt/motion/join/runtime` interfaces and then forwards dozens of fields into one `useGenerateBatch(...)` call, mixing UI state, task request shape, and join-workflow controls in one seam.

Submission orchestration remains split across multiple hook layers (`useFormSubmission` -> `useSubmissionHandlers` -> prompt queue handlers), with queue-specific paths redoing task-param building and task-runner wiring instead of using one cohesive command surface.

Pane lifecycle behavior is still duplicated between `TasksPane` and `GenerationsPane` (separate pointer-event delays, pane-open guards, local interaction gating), indicating shared interaction policy is encoded per-pane rather than centrally.

Lightbox split improved rendering separation but still depends on a broad shared facade: `useSharedLightboxState` (735 lines) composes many concerns and returns a very wide object that both `ImageLightbox` and `VideoLightbox` must adapt around.

Legacy adapters in src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts convert structured OperationResult failures into false/undefined (lines 315-327), discarding errorCode/policy metadata defined in src/shared/lib/operationResult.ts.

supabase/functions/_shared/billing.ts throws generic Error strings for canonical/legacy sub-task lookup failures (lines 145-176), and supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts collapses all such failures into subtask_query_failed 500 responses (lines 392-399).

supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts intentionally fail-opens on RPC/runtime errors (lines 78-119) but returns a normal allowed response shape without a degraded marker, unlike explicit degraded/errorCode signaling used in src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts (lines 114-119).

Previously flagged task-shape misclassification appears fixed in scope: supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts now returns task_shape_invalid with 422 on parse failures (lines 337-344), rather than masking as 404.
error_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Error handling contracts remain fragmented at several boundaries (OperationResult -> boolean adapters, generic Error wrapping in billing helpers, and fail-open rate-limit responses without explicit degraded state), which reduces diagnosability and makes failure behavior less predictable.

findings.summary: Image and video lightbox flows still maintain forked render-model assembly contracts, which increases drift and integration blast radius.
evidence: Both branches independently construct broad memoized seam objects (`lightboxStateValue`, `layoutProps`) and then feed the same layout shell.
Image flow owns local environment/shared-state/render assembly; video flow does equivalent assembly through separate controller/render-model hooks.
Shared layout depends on a wide context contract, so any contract change must be mirrored across both assembly paths.
findings.suggestion: Introduce one canonical lightbox controller contract (core/media/variants/navigation/workflow/controls) with shared builders for common sections, then keep image/video-only fields in narrow extension objects consumed by `LightboxLayout`.

findings.summary: Individual travel-segment task creation still bypasses the typed task-input seam with a double cast when reading resolution.
evidence: `createIndividualTravelSegmentTask` reads `parsed_resolution_wh` as `asString((params as unknown as Record<string, unknown>).parsed_resolution_wh)`.
The same module and adjacent task modules already use parser helpers (`toRecordOrEmpty`, `asString`, payload snapshots/contracts) for boundary normalization.
This cast sidesteps compile-time shape guarantees and can silently hide contract drift between task creation and completion readers.
findings.suggestion: Remove the cast by adding `parsed_resolution_wh?: string` to the segment input type (or by reading from a normalized record built once), and standardize this resolution extraction through one shared reader utility used by task creators.

findings.summary: Inline edit creates a third integration seam by reassembling lightbox editing primitives outside the main lightbox controller path.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` orchestrates many hooks sourced from `MediaLightbox/hooks` (upscale, inpainting, magic edit, reposition, img2img, settings sync).
`InlineEditView` directly composes `MediaDisplayWithCanvas`, control groups, and `EditModePanel`, duplicating layout/integration responsibilities also present in lightbox flows.
This produces three active handoff surfaces for similar edit behavior (image lightbox, video lightbox, inline edit), increasing maintenance and behavior skew risk.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared image-edit surface controller + presenter contract (mode state, commands, derived UI model) and have both inline edit and image lightbox consume it, leaving only container-specific layout wrappers.

findings.summary: Task creation seam still bypasses compile-time contract via double cast.
evidence: `createTravelBetweenImagesTask(requestBody as unknown as TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput)` is still present at `generateVideoService.ts:724`.
The request object is assembled ad hoc across helper builders, then force-cast at the boundary rather than validated against the canonical task input type.
findings.suggestion: Replace the cast with a typed builder/validator that returns `TravelBetweenImagesTaskInput` (or a typed failure) before submission; add a focused unit test that fails when required task-contract fields are missing or wrong-shaped.

findings.summary: Billing trigger still accepts malformed 2xx payloads as success if they are object-shaped.
evidence: In `billing.ts:246-281`, 2xx handling only rejects non-object payloads or explicit `error/errorCode`; otherwise it returns `{ ok: true }` even if required success semantics are absent.
Current tests (`billing.test.ts:125-205`) cover happy path and status-code failures but do not assert rejection of malformed object success envelopes.
findings.suggestion: Require an explicit success contract for 2xx responses (for example, `skipped === true` or finite numeric `cost`, plus optional stable marker), return `cost_calculation_invalid_response` when missing, and add regression tests for `{}` and unrelated object payloads.

findings.summary: Rate-limit seam trusts RPC payload shape and can produce invalid enforcement metadata.
evidence: `rateLimit.ts:90-108` casts `data` to `{ allowed; count; reset_at }` and immediately uses fields without runtime checks.

Malformed RPC payloads can yield invalid `Date`/`NaN` retry values or misclassified `allowed` decisions, and existing tests only cover thrown RPC errors plus static 429 response formatting (`rateLimit.test.ts:10-47`).
findings.suggestion: Introduce a runtime parser for RPC result shape with finite-value checks; on invalid payload, fail open with a structured diagnostic path (or explicit policy branch) and add tests for malformed `data` objects.

findings.summary: Different payload readers use divergent precedence rules, causing inconsistent cross-layer behavior.
evidence: `pickTaskDetailsString` (`normalizeTaskDetailsPayload.ts:45-47`) resolves `parsedParams -> orchestratorDetails -> orchestratorPayload`.
`travelPayloadReader` defaults to `taskViewContract -> individualSegmentParams -> familyContract -> orchestratorDetails -> fullOrchestratorPayload -> rawParams` (`travelPayloadReader.ts:21-28`).
Both modules read semantically overlapping fields but from different source-order contracts, so UI detail rendering can diverge from task-read logic after migrations.
findings.suggestion: Define one shared payload-resolution precedence utility and reuse it in both task-details and travel readers; add contract tests that assert identical value resolution for mixed legacy/canonical payloads.

findings.summary: Generation controller still forwards a multi-domain config surface instead of a cohesive generation contract.
evidence: `useGenerationController` maintains five separate interface groups (`core`, `prompt`, `motion`, `join`, `runtime`) and then flattens many fields into one `useGenerateBatch(...)` invocation.
The call site mixes unrelated concerns (prompt text, motion/phase tuning, structure-video data, join/stitch policy, runtime UI flags), so the interface boundary is not domain-honest.
`useEditingController` similarly returns broad `mediaEditing` and `joinWorkflow` bundles, reinforcing bag-style cross-feature handoff rather than narrow domain commands.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a typed request-model builder in the controller layer (for example `buildBatchGenerationRequest` and `buildJoinGenerationRequest`) and pass only those models plus minimal runtime callbacks to execution hooks.

findings.summary: Form submission behavior remains fragmented across hook layers with repeated queue/task orchestration patterns.
evidence: `useFormSubmission` captures a mutable snapshot and separately wires a task-param builder before delegating orchestration, creating multiple coordination seams for one workflow.
`useSubmissionHandlers` fans submission into separate handler families rather than exposing one command-oriented orchestrator API.

`usePromptQueueHandlers` repeats orchestration scaffolding across actions (task param lookup, `automatedSubmitButton.trigger()`, `runIncomingTask(...)`, execute callback wrapping), indicating duplicated workflow mechanics.
findings.suggestion: Collapse submission paths into one orchestrator hook/service that owns validation, prompt transformation, task-param construction, and incoming-task execution; expose explicit commands (`submitManaged`, `queueExisting`, `queueLikeExisting`) backed by shared internals.

findings.summary: Sliding pane interaction lifecycle is still duplicated across pane implementations.
evidence: Both pane components implement their own pointer-event deferral with 300ms timers after open, instead of one shared lifecycle policy.
`GenerationsPane` adds extra local interaction gating and dropdown reset timing that mirrors pane-open synchronization concerns already present elsewhere.
`PanesContext` centralizes lock/open state, but timing and interaction guards remain distributed in pane components, leaving behavior consistency to per-pane implementation.
findings.suggestion: Extract shared pane lifecycle primitives (for example `usePaneInteractionLifecycle`) that handle open transition timing, pointer gating, and transient interaction disablement; keep each pane focused on content and pane-specific actions.

findings.summary: Lightbox split still depends on a large shared state facade with broad prop and return surfaces.
evidence: `useSharedLightboxState.ts` is 735 lines and composes many concerns (variants, navigation, star, references, lineage, shot management, source generation, layout, button props) behind one hook return contract.
`MediaLightbox` still exposes a large flat prop surface, then rebundles it via `useBundledLightboxProps` before dispatching to image/video components.

`ImageLightbox` and `VideoLightbox` both depend on grouped shared contracts (`navigation`, `shotWorkflow`, `features`, `actions`), indicating the split retained a central broad facade rather than capability-focused seams.
findings.suggestion: Break the shared facade into smaller capability hooks consumed directly by image/video flows (for example variants, shot actions, references/join, layout), and replace flat parent props with narrower feature-specific prop contracts.

findings.summary: Shot editor legacy adapters still flatten OperationResult failures into boolean/undefined return values.
evidence: runAddToShotOperation/runCreateShotOperation produce OperationResult failures with errorCode/policy/recoverable metadata.
handleAddToShotLegacyAdapter and handleAddToShotWithoutPositionLegacyAdapter return false when result.ok is false (lines 315-323), and handleCreateShotLegacyAdapter returns undefined (lines 325-327).
operationResult.ts defines preserved failure metadata and a conversion helper (toOperationResultError) that these adapters bypass.
findings.suggestion: Keep structured failures across the legacy boundary (e.g., return OperationResult from consumers) or convert failures to OperationResultError via toOperationResultError and throw, so callers can branch on errorCode/policy instead of ambiguous false/undefined.

findings.summary: Sub-task lookup errors are wrapped as generic Error strings and then collapsed to one edge error code, reducing root-cause fidelity.
evidence: lookupCompletedSubTasksForOrchestrator throws new Error("[Billing] Failed to query ...") for both canonical and legacy query failures (billing.ts lines 145-176), without structured error code or preserved cause field.

calculate-task-cost catches any thrown lookup error and always returns subtask_query_failed (index.ts lines 392-399), so downstream cannot distinguish canonical query failure, legacy fallback query failure, or transport/runtime faults.
findings.suggestion: Return/throw a typed error object from billing lookup with explicit source_stage (canonical|legacy), errorCode, and cause; map those fields directly in calculate-task-cost responses/logging to preserve actionable failure class.

findings.summary: Rate-limit infrastructure failures silently degrade to allowed requests without machine-readable degraded signaling.
evidence: checkRateLimit returns allowed:true on RPC error and catch paths (rateLimit.ts lines 78-119), with only console.error side effects.
Returned shape is indistinguishable from normal success except remaining/reset values, so callers cannot programmatically detect degraded enforcement.
Other fail-open paths in scope expose explicit degraded metadata (e.g., calculateDerivedCountsSafe returns degraded:true and errorCode:'query_failed' in generationTransformers.ts lines 114-119).
findings.suggestion: Add explicit degraded metadata to RateLimitResult (e.g., degraded:true plus reason/errorCode) and surface it via headers/log fields so callers and observability pipelines can detect and alert on disabled enforcement.
